title: Holsten Residential District
description: Located on the site of Hamburg's historic Holsten brewery, this competition proposal reimagines a city block as a sequence of distinct residential parcels — each with its own character, yet coherent as a whole.

title: District Administration Extension — Hybrid Timber
description: The District Administration Extension builds upon the site’s historic fabric with quiet confidence—bridging past and present through clarity, proportion, and light. Its considered volume maintains architectural coherence while allowing for future growth. Inside, the building is shaped as a contemporary workplace designed for connection and adaptability. Natural light defines the spatial rhythm, while an open timber staircase encourages movement and informal exchange across levels. A mix of single and double offices is complemented by flexible meeting and support areas—spaces that balance focus and collaboration. Built in a hybrid timber–concrete structure, the project follows Cradle to Cradle principles, reducing embodied carbon and allowing materials to be reversibly connected and reused. Prefabrication ensured precision and efficiency on site, while the warm, tactile interior enhances everyday comfort and wellbeing. The result is an environment of clarity, care, and continuity—a calm, enduring setting that reflects our studio’s belief that good design connects people and context through spaces made to last.

title: Sophienschule Campus
description: The school is imagined as a living learning landscape—a place where education unfolds through movement, encounter, and quiet reflection. Its architecture balances structure and spontaneity, creating spaces that invite curiosity and belonging. A central courtyard forms the heart of the building, linking classrooms and shared areas while drawing nature into everyday life. Inside, an open staircase becomes a vertical commons—part circulation, part stage, part gathering space—encouraging moments of exchange throughout the day. The building’s hybrid timber structure expresses warmth and sustainability, supporting a calm interior defined by natural light, texture, and acoustic comfort. Together, these elements foster a sense of wellbeing that supports both focus and play. At its centre, the Room of Stillness offers a counterpoint—a space for pause and introspection. Inspired by natural grotto formations, its sculpted timber ceiling and enveloping wooden surfaces create an immersive, meditative environment where students and teachers can retreat and reconnect. The result is a school that feels alive, generous, and deeply human—a place that learns and grows alongside its community.

title: Cathedral Museum & Monastery Renewal
description: The historic monastery building adjacent to the cathedral cloister has a rich history which our careful design restoration and revitalization celebrates. Initially serving as a retirement and nursing home until 2007, careful planning explored various potential uses in 2011 - ultimately leading to a parish hall and offices for the Parish of St. Peter in 2017. Under the supervision of the parish, with generous support from the Diocese of Fulda and public donors, the monastery is undergoing thorough renovation and partial reconstruction. Simultaneously, the cathedral museum has been revamped with new exhibition equipment and climate-controlled galleries - while the museum library received upgraded displays and technical equipment for better preservation.Outside, a new barrier-free entrance and forecourt improves access, as well as providing an open space for community gatherings. Following conservation guidelines, the facade and roof underwent careful renovation, preserving their historical significance. Limited interventions ensure compliance, allowing for respectful restoration while honoring the building's heritage.

title: Sartorius Quartier
description: On the former industrial site and factory premises of Sartorius AG in the northern part of Göttingen, a new mixed-use district is brough to life, jointly developed by HAMBURG TEAM and Sartorius AG. Three historic buildings on site have been retained and refurbished and together with an open hall and a new building, will form the new district. The district includes residential buildings, daycare center, commercial spaces along with a hotel with a restaurant and connecting retail space. Additionally, a new concept of smart- serviced apartments with be at the center of the distric, while a rooftop Skybar brings life to the area with unmatchable views of the city. This visionary project aims to blend history with innovation, fostering a dynamic environment for work, life, and leisure in the heart of Göttingen.The project has been awarded the Brownfield Prize, for it's positive tranformation from an unused industrial site to a lively neighbourhood.

title: Façade Revitalization
description: Our proposal for revitalizing the façade of a corporate headquarters blends timelessness with modern sustainability, prioritizing durability and energy efficiency. Focused on meeting our client's needs and reflecting their identity, the design strives for both aesthetic impact and functionality. A highlight of the project is the energy-optimized brick façade. High-quality bricks, carefully chosen for their thermal properties, enhance insulation, reducing energy consumption significantly compared to the current structure. We prioritize a clear and welcoming ground-floor entrance, essential for a corporate setting, by optimizing traffic flow and removing barriers. Strategic placements of glass cubes as interventions add contemporary flair and natural light, creating focal points that enhance the overall aesthetic and functionality. Revitalizing architectural landmarks brings myriad benefits, from improved energy efficiency to enhanced aesthetics, ultimately creating spaces that inspire and contribute positively to occupants and their surroundings.

title: Office & Kindergarten Pavilion
description: Our response to the competition brief, focuses on the addition of a new building, housing a day-care center and flexible offices. Drawing inspiration from adjacent buildings, our proposal completed the development of the company's campus. A key focus of the office floors is to create contemporary workspaces that are flexible, well-lit, and conducive to communication. The layout features single, double, and team offices flanking a versatile multi-functional zone, comprising meeting rooms, waiting areas and suporting spaces. We prioritize access to outdoor spaces to enhance well-being - including terraces, balconies and courtyards on every floor. The integration of the kindergarden at the ground floor enables flexibility for working parents. Densely planted areas and the use of softer materials like wood create a safe environment for the children. The design proposal fosters a family-friendly environment within the workplace, promoting a better work-life balance.

title: Bergedorf District
description: The brief seeks an innovative urban development strategy for Bergedorf's central shopping streets, revitalizing currently abandoned spaces. The design proposes a cohesive yet distinctive vision for the 2 plots in question. Drawing inspiration from the existing urban fabric, the proposed buildings maintain a harmonious scale and structure, with additional like public offerings and roof terraces. Ground floors are designed with spacious, open-plan retail areas, acting as an extension of the surrounding park and plazas. The proposed apartment building offers an inner courtyard and rooftop gardens for a serene green spaces for relaxation and play. Enhancements extend beyond the buildings, with urban interventions redesigned to promote connectivity - prioritizing pedestrian comfort and access while maintaining convenience for motorists and cyclists. Kaiser-Wilhelm-Plaza is envisioned as a welcoming green space, complementing the castle garden and offering potential for dining, events, and markets. Through thoughtful urban planning, the project aims to enhance Bergedorf's charm and functionality, creating inviting spaces for residents and visitors alike.
client: D-H3 and Trei Real Estate in partnership with city of Hamburg

title: Primary School Community Hub
description: Our studio focuses on designing a new community center for an existing primary school, addressing the need for a larger cafeteria, modern kindergarten, and dynamic youth center. Centered on innovation and functionality, our proposal elevates the school campus while setting a new standard for neighborhood infrastructure.Key features include a central gathering space seamlessly integrated with the entrance foyer and the childcare center, providing convenient access to terrace-like outdoor area to be used by the local community. Efficiency is prioritized through prefabricated construction elements, while sustainability is ensured with mineral construction and a hybrid support structure. Throughout the process, adaptability remains paramount, facilitating seamless modifications to meet evolving needs.

title: Geometries of Physics: Structural Networks Workshop
description: Led by our Directors, this academic research workshop starts with a simple premise: How much does your model weigh? How much load can it bear? The objective is to delve into the exploration of intuitive, load-bearing networks, bridging analogue and digital methodologies to innovate in modularity and connectivity. Inspired by historical pioneers such as Gaudí and their gravity-based form-finding methods, participants will begin by experimenting with hanging chain models and load tracing studies to uncover natural structural principles. The discovered networks will then be materialized using techniques rooted in stereotomy, including the use of ruled surfaces, clay block formations, and robotic foam wire cutting. Through iterative stress testing and cataloging, the workshop emphasizes achieving an optimal balance between material efficiency and load capacity. This hands-on exploration lays the groundwork for scalable architectural solutions that challenge conventional construction approaches and celebrate the synergy of physics and design.

title: Neue Mitte — Urban Renewal
description: The city faces a persistent housing demand amidst limited land availability, our design approch for this brief focuses on revitalizing underutilized areas to help aliviate the current situation. In the South district, the former pension office site stands as a prime candidate for urban renewal - currently vacant and deteriorating, the building awaits transformation. The district's existing structures, including the perimeter block along Frankfurter street and housing developments along Rembrandt street, converge on the site, shaping the vision for the "New Center". The proposed disctrict utilizes height variations, public ground floors as well as private courtyards and green spaces, to enhance the quality of living. The district achieves the target metrics while fostering pedestrian connectivity and visual transparency, contributing to a vibrant and cohesive urban fabric.
client: Nassauische Heimstätte Wohnungs- und Entwicklungsgesellschaft mbH

title: Fuhle 101 Mixed-Use
description: Located in Hamburg’s vibrant Barmbek district, this mixed-use building is conceived as a contemporary urban landmark—a place that brings together living, working, and leisure within one cohesive setting. The design accommodates a diverse mix of uses—from retail and dining to offices, a hotel, fitness centre, and neighbourhood garage—creating a daily hub for the surrounding community. Transparency and permeability at street level foster activity and engagement, while upper levels offer a sense of calm and order above the bustle. The masonry façade combines refinement and familiarity, reinterpreting Hamburg’s red-brick tradition through vertical proportions and subtle textural variation. Its layered rhythm gives the building depth and tactility, responding to light and movement throughout the day. A gently set-back volume ensures continuity with neighbouring structures, while generous shopfronts and a sheltered arcade create an inviting public edge—a place that feels both civic and human in scale. It reflects our studio’s belief that good urban architecture balances permanence, openness, and everyday ease—grounded in context and crafted for people.

title: Europacity Neighbourhood
description: Rooted in a vision of high-quality urban living, the project reimagines neighbourhood life through small-scale volumes that foster diversity, intimacy, and a strong sense of community. A series of courtyards opens the site, connecting private and semi-public spaces while weaving together the rhythms of everyday life. Each plot is designed according to its specific location within the masterplan, with tailored frontages, subdivided units, and setback entrances that create variation and human scale. Open ground floors accommodate local businesses, encouraging activity and a thriving street atmosphere.At the heart of the development, two connected courtyard squares prioritise residents, offering shared spaces for gathering and play. Toward the southeast, the plan opens to the public realm, inviting engagement and extending the sense of community beyond the site itself. The result is an urban fabric that balances density and openness—designed to transform how people live, meet, and belong in the city.
collaborators:
Design Area 12.4: Bieling Architects

Design Area 11.2, 11.3, 11.4, 12.5, 12.1, 12.2: Max Dudler

Design Area 12.3., 11.1: Lorenzen Mayer Architects

Execution of all construction areas: Bieling Architects

title: Bellevue Apartments
description: Located along Hamburg’s scenic Outer Alster Lake, this residential building completes a historic perimeter block with a design that bridges heritage and modernity. Framed by listed neighbours marked by projections and recesses, the new addition integrates seamlessly—respectful in tone yet distinctly contemporary. The light limestone façade is finely detailed, with horizontal natural stone bands that subtly reference local tradition. Bronze-framed windows lend depth and warmth, their rhythm creating a refined play of proportion and shadow across the surface. A stepped-back penthouse with a generous roof terrace offers panoramic views of the lake, forming a calm and elegant conclusion to the building’s silhouette. Inside, careful spatial planning ensures bright, well-proportioned interiors that balance openness with privacy. The project reflects our studio’s belief that contemporary living can coexist gracefully within historic fabric—through clarity, material care, and a quiet sense of belonging.

title: Seminary Chapel
description: Set within the historic seminary campus, the Seminary Church serves as a contemporary sanctuary—bridging heritage and modern expression through calm proportion and material clarity. Its pure white form conveys simplicity and reverence, while subtle detailing invites a quiet sense of awe. At the centre lies the stone “carpet”, a symbolic pathway extending from the courtyard into the chapel. This gesture links sacred and everyday space, guiding movement and reflection. Inside, the altar—crafted from Dietfurt limestone—anchors the interior with tactile permanence, expressing the spiritual depth of its ritual purpose. Above, diamond-patterned timber slats diffuse light with warmth and softness, their rhythm harmonising with the glow from expansive glazing. Every tactile element has been crafted in collaboration with local artisans, whose precision and care imbue the space with a grounded calmness. The interplay of stone, wood, and light creates a setting of profound balance—a timeless space for worship, contemplation, and connection.

title: WIKUS Headquarters & Conference Centre
description: The new WIKUS Headquarters marks a distinct architectural identity for the global leader in precision saw technology—translating technical excellence into an expressive architectural form. A new entrance building and conference centre define the campus’s focal point, welcoming visitors and employees alike. The flowing geometry of the façade gives the building a sense of movement and intent, while the folding façade—a reinterpretation of the company’s signature saw blade—creates a dynamic, kinetic surface that embodies both innovation and craft. The façade's perforated panels modulate transparency and daylight, ensuring visual lightness and adaptable comfort throughout the day. The design unifies existing and new structures, establishing a coherent architectural presence that reflects both continuity and progress. Inaugurated on the company’s 60th anniversary, the project stands as a built expression of precision, innovation, and human ingenuity—designed to connect identity, function, and experience into one seamless whole.

title: Papiermühlengasse Urban Block
description: The design is rooted in the urban planning principle of urban blocks, forming the backbone of the city's layout. At our competition site, this principle is enhanced by creating a prominent corner entrance while allowing for open spaces for plazas. The program distribution at the groundfloor focuses on both users and visitors, offering a mix of retail and gastronomy. The landscape and open spaces, guide the users through a space sequence of the main entrance and into the inner courtyard. The courtyard serves as a vibrant and serene green space away from the bustling train station adjecent.

title: Catholic University Auditorium & Seminar Pavilion
description: The Catholic University of Applied Sciences is enhancing its academic environment to accommodate its growing student body. Addressing the pressing need for additional lecture halls and seminar rooms, we designed and delivered a new single-story building seamlessly integrating into the historic charm of the campus and its lush parkland setting. Designed to harmonize with the existing architecture, the new structure delicately rises above ground level, exuding an aura of lightness amidst the solidity of surrounding walls and portals. Accessible from both the park and the main building, the new facility prioritizes inclusivity with barrier-free entrances. Inside, wooden feautures provide warmth and acoustic comfort. Floor-to-ceiling glass panels on its facades infuse teaching spaces with natural light while versatile partition walls ensure flexibility in teaching methodologies. Constructed primarily from sustainable materials, including renewable wood for structural elements, the building embraces eco-conscious design. All elements come together creating a serene and conducive environment for learning and collaboration.

title: Robotic Fibre-Weaving Canopy
description: This project investigates the innovative use of robotic fiber weaving in conjunction with flat-packed wood structures. The process begins with on-site assembly of the wood components, utilizing snap-in-place joints to create a precise three-dimensional framework. Within this framework, robotic weaving technology is employed to intricately craft the canopy, translating complex geometries into a cohesive and functional design. The synergy between the flat-packed structure and robotic weaving allows for the creation of efficient, yet aesthetically striking forms that challenge traditional construction practices. By merging material efficiency with structural integrity, this project serves as a foundation for future exploration of scalable, unique architectural solutions, demonstrating the vast potential of robotic fiber weaving in modern design.

title: Fabric to Form: Lycra Casting Research
description: The project exploresthe transformation of 2D Lycra patterns into 3D plaster structures, enhancing the understanding of material behavior and structural integrity. This innovative approach demonstrates a unique method of material experimentation, showcasing cutting-edge design techniques. By developing and applying specific stitching patterns to Lycra, the process uses gravitational forces during plaster casting to morph a flat plane into a curved surface. This method controls plaster flow and material deformation, creating aesthetically appealing and structurally sound forms. The investigation involved five explorations of curvature, starting with simple 2D curves and progressing to complex 3D shapes with points of torsion. The project underscores a commitment to pushing the boundaries of traditional design, providing new insights into material capabilities and design.

title: Robotic Clay Printing: Form & Code
description: This project focuses on harnessing the potential of robotic clay printing in architecture. We analyze natural phenomena, breaking them down into their geometric characteristics and behaviors. These attributes are then translated into code, generating a series of graphs that inherently consider the possibilities of robotic clay printing three-dimensional structures. The precision and versatility of 3D printing allow for the creation of complex forms that would be challenging or impossible to achieve through traditional methods. This technology not only enhances design flexibility but also accelerates the development process, enabling rapid prototyping and refinement. Ultimately, the goal is to demonstrate the transformative potential of robotic clay printing in architectural innovation and efficiency, pushing the boundaries of what’s possible in modern design.

title: Schalles House — Mixed-Use
description: Amidst the urban fabric, our building emerges as a striking presence, seamlessly integrating with the surrounding architectural context. Encased in a sturdy brick facade, the building exudes solidity and permanence, yet welcomes abundant natural light through expansive windows that illuminate its interiors. On its southern aspect, a glass facade maximizes sunlight across the floors, while inset loggias offer secluded outdoor retreats for residents. Crafted from robust reinforced concrete and masonry, the building's exterior not only lends structural integrity but also fosters adaptability, facilitating a versatile blend of mixed-use spaces. Inside, the office areas are designed for dynamic collaboration, with open layouts tailored to accommodate diverse business needs. Meanwhile, the residential units are thoughtfully curated, offering a range of sizes to accommodate various family configurations, ensuring a comfortable and personalized living experience for all.

title: Motel One
description: The design vision integrates the building seamlessly into the fabric of city's old town, drawing inspiration from the traditional pitched roof typology. Reflecting the intimate scale of the historic center, the building is composed of three adjacent volumes. The pitched roof front facing Markttwiete establishes crucial urban connections between the surrounding streetscape and the town hall square. An elegant two-story arcade, reminiscent of the historic town hall arcade, enhances the streetscape continuity. This configuration strengthens the spatial relationship with the market square, enhancing its prominence. Our design proposes a brick facade featuring a range of textures, from smooth surfaces to intricate three-dimensional arrangements, adding visual interest and depth to the building's profile.

title: Kassel-Calden Airport Terminal
description: The transformation of the current airfield into a full-fledged airport encompasses several key developments. These include the creation of a new runway and parallel taxiway, alongside essential facilities such as a terminal building, control tower, firefighting stations, and maintenance hangars. Future plans include the provision of a general aviation terminal and a larger commercial zone catering to future aviation needs. This expansion aims to facilitate regular tourist, low-cost, and business flights while bolstering existing commercial activities associated with the airport. The terminal design emphasizes efficiency, featuring a compact two-story structure. Notably, the design allows for potential future expansion, with provisions for extending the building longitudinally on both sides. The terminal layout comprises three levels: Level 0, situated at apron level and accessible from the airside; Level 1, the primary terminal level accessible from landside; and Level 2, accommodating technical and ancillary functions, seamlessly integrated for easy accessibility.

title: Hybrid House — Live/Work
description: The design presents a standalone volume within the master plan, featuring cuts, interruptions, and setbacks that break up the cube. Deep incisions on all façade sides allow natural light to permeate the interior, creating niches that serve as balconies, providing residents with sheltered outdoor spaces. Embracing a hybrid usage concept, our design seamlessly integrates residential and commercial elements, promoting a cohesive living and working environment while preserving distinct zones for optimal comfort and productivity. Adhering to a flexible layout, each floor offers a diverse range of spaces, from individual units to expansive duplexes, catering to various lifestyle needs. Emphasizing functionality, we've meticulously planned separate entrances for office and residential domains, ensuring seamless navigation for all occupants. Inside, a dual-flight staircase layout mitigates any potential conflicts between residential and business users, fostering a harmonious coexistence.
client: Deutsche Real Estate and Hamburg + Wernst Real Estate

title: Albert-Schweitzer-School Student Research Center
description: The new building at Albert-Schweitzer-School in Kassel offers a prime location bridging the schoolyard and the main street. With entrances on both levels, it ensures convenient access for students and visitors. The staggered design adapts seamlessly to the varied terrain, maximizing functionality while enhancing the aesthetic appeal. Constructed with aluminum perforated sheeting, the façade strikes a balance between energy efficiency and visual distinction, signaling the building's unique purpose. Inside, versatile spaces cater to diverse needs, from student workshops to classrooms and labs. This layout fosters collaborative learning and efficient supervision. Notably, the rooftop observatory serves as the heart of the student research center, inspiring curiosity and scientific exploration. Overall, this project not only meets practical needs but also promotes innovation and educational excellence, making it a valuable asset for the school and its community.

title: Fürstenriedstraße Commercial
description: At the core of our vision lies the creation a commercial hub that harmonizes with its urban surroundings and meets the diverse needs of a prime retail and office space. The ground level of our new building is embraced by expansive glass panels, inviting in natural light and showcasing vibrant activity within. Above, an array of advertising displays adorns the facade, ensuring visibility and engagement. Meanwhile, a mix of sleek vertical lines and textured metal accents infuse the structure with modern elegance, resonating with the bustling energy of office life on the upper floors. As the building extends into the property, it seamlessly integrates with the surrounding residential landscape. Inside, spacious layouts offer possibilities for adaptable use, ensuring enduring relevance and functionality. By thoughtfully optimizing every space, we maximize the potential for dynamic retail experiences while ensuring a welcoming atmosphere flooded with daylight.

title: Packhofstraße Corner Commercial
description: The creation of a vibrant 5-story commercial hub is perfectly situated to meet the demands of a bustling downtown environment. Inspired by the surrounding urban landscape, our design seamlessly blends modern functionality with timeless aesthetic. By strategically incorporating elements like ground-level storefronts and expansive glass features, we've crafted a space that invites foot traffic and draws attention businesses. With its prominent corner location, the building not only stands as a landmark but also serves as a gateway to the thriving Heiliger street. By maximizing visibility and accessibility, our design ensures that businesses enjoy prime exposure, driving growth and success. From its adaptable layout to its harmonious integration with neighboring structures, every aspect of this project is tailored to elevate presence in the vibrant downtown scene.

title: 105 Große Bockenheimer Straße — Mixed-Use
description: Our new development on Frankfurt's bustling Bockenheimer street, offers a prime blend of retail, office, and residential spaces. With a strategic zoning approach, the facade elegantly divides into distinct sections, catering to diverse needs. From ground-level retail to upper-floor offices and exclusive residences, each area is thoughtfully designed for optimal functionality and aesthetic appeal. The facade's glass fiber concrete panels not only ensure structural integrity but also harmonize with the urban landscape. Additionally, the residential floors feature terraced walkways, enhancing their quality of life while maintaining independence. This thoughtful design ensures a seamless blend of functionality and privacy for occupants amidst the city buzz.

title: Wallhöfe Mixed-Use Quarter
description: The redevelopment of the area introduces a vibrant mix of living and working spaces. Emphasizing urban blocks, plazas, and streets, the design fosters a dynamic environment for high-quality living and working experiences. Our design strategy aims to revitalize the surrounding urban fabric, restoring the traditional block structure while creating inviting spaces for residents and workers alike. By enclosing outer edges and creating internal pathways, the development prioritizes community interaction and enhances individual living and working environments. The project includes an eleven-story hotel tower with a connecting extension, an office building spanning seven stories, a boardinghouse offering diverse room sizes, and five-story residential buildings surrounding a central square. Ground-level retail further enriches the neighborhood's vibrancy, enhancing the overall quality of life for residents and visitors.
client: Neuer Steinweg, Deutsche Real Estate and Hamburg Team

title: QVC Customer Contact Campus
description: The new QVC Call Center stands as a testament to urban integration and functional design. Nestled between Stern- and Leipziger Streets, its innovative architecture harmonizes with the surrounding cityscape. The building's curved façade and elongated windows not only create a visually striking presence but also maximize natural light and ventilation within. Its strategic location, fosters connectivity and convenience for both employees and visitors. Moreover, the inclusion of an above-ground parking facility addresses practical needs while minimizing disruptions to the urban environment. Inside, an open staircase fosters seamless movement across floors, promoting collaboration and efficiency. Additionally, the second-floor courtyard provides a tranquil retreat for employees, enhancing their well-being and productivity. Overall, the QVC Call Center exemplifies how considered design can elevate urban spaces and enrich the lives of those within them.

title: Basler Securitas Offices
description: Located near Bad Homburg's train station and downtown, this project offers modern office spaces designed for optimal productivity and collaboration. Its innovative architecture features two interconnected cubic structures, promoting a dynamic work environment. With a focus on natural light and flexible layouts, the building prioritizes employee well-being. Spacious lobbies, meeting points on each floor, and lounge-like tea-kitchens encourage interaction and creativity. Artist Jan Albers' designs adorn the entrance hall and meeting points, adding a unique touch to the space. Overall, this project provides a vibrant and functional workspace, enhancing both productivity and employee satisfaction.

title: Druseltalstraße — Mixed-Use
description: The project thrives in its thoughtful design and strategic placement within the urban landscape. By embracing a simple yet elegant cubic form, the volume is a timeless and highly functional. The distinctive elliptical staircase tower not only serves as a focal point but also guides visitors to the main entrance, enhancing the user experience. Moreover, the integration of a standalone community room and residential units offers use versatility, catering to diverse needs within the community. This multifaceted approach enhances the building's utility and contributes to its significance as a hub for both business and residential purposes. Overall, the project's emphasis on practicality, accessibility, and versatility makes it an integral part of the urban fabric.

title: Deutsche Bahn Offices — Railside Campus
description: The comb-like structure of the new office building stems from its urban context. The long five-story building forms a spine on the railway-facing side, mirroring the curve of the railway line. The priority is to design bright office spaces that are flexible and offer optimal working conditions. This is achieved largely through a large, double-layered soundproof facade to mitigate sound from the adjecent railway tracks. On the east side, transparent structures alternate with small courtyards, echoing the neighborhood's diversity. The building block features perforated facades, extending their color scheme into the interior and creating a subtle glow through the glass facades of the courtyard views.

title: K+S Headquarters
description: The urban concept and architectural design enhance the boulevard-like nature of the street. As the company's headquarters, the volume offers a distinct curved metal facade in combination with a light-colored natural stone. A transparent glass facade with closed parapets creates a sense of lightness in the courtyard area. Inside, the office spaces are luminous and spacious, fostering a flexible and comfortable working environment. The building's form reflects its qualities, embodying functionality and aesthetic appeal.

title: St. Isidor Church
description: The church is strategically positioned at the highest point of the site, with its entrance facing the town center. The arrangement of gently expanding walls exudes an inviting ambiance. Together with the tower, they create a sheltered church square. The tower, standing in axial alignment with the church entrance, serves as a beacon, consciously noticed upon entering the foyer. The foyer itself is a tranquil space for reflection, characterized by its height and square layout. The design prioritizes accessibility and serenity, offering visitors a welcoming and contemplative environment from the moment they arrive.

title: St. Hubertus Sacristy
description: The new sacristy complements the historic ensemble of the churchyard. Its facade, echoing the neighboring parish church in materials and architectural form, envelops the entire structure with a skin of porphyry. A subtle canopy connects the buildings, providing a sheltered passage from the sacristy to the church. By aligning the sacristy parallel to the church's nave, a serene courtyard is formed, offering space for parish gatherings. The courtyard's paving highlights the historical foundations of the church, adding depth to the site's historical significance. This addition not only enhances the architectural harmony of the church complex but also enriches its functionality, providing a welcoming space for congregational activities.

title: Rathgeber Residences
description: The residential new build embodies a harmonious blend of tradition and modernity. By respecting the architectural heritage while introducing contemporary elements, the project adds aesthetic value to the community, enhancing its appeal and character. The development optimizes land use without compromising on quality of life with generous apartments and matching green spaces. The design is characterized by a seamless integration of indoor and outdoor spaces, promoting a sense of connection with nature while ensuring privacy and comfort for residents. The project carefully considers the preservation of existing trees, as a commitment to creating a sustainable and livable environment. The residential new build not only meets the needs of modern living but also enriches the urban fabric of the district.

title: Ambulatory Cardiac Centre
description: The project boasts a distinct architectural approach, with its L-shaped base, clad in fiber cement panels, supports a striking glass upper body adorned with aluminum panels, creating a visually captivating structure. The building efficiently houses the cardiac clinic facilities on its lower floors and examination rooms in the glass cube facing the street, optimizing space and functionality. Inside, warm wood surfaces and colored walls foster a comforting atmosphere, enhancing patient experience. Under a glass roof, the airy waiting area features cantilevered galleries and a modern staircase, promoting a sense of openness and lightness. This project's recognition with the first prize at the German Facade Award in 2001 underscores its architectural ingenuity and contribution to urban design excellence.
client: Dr. A. Utech and Dr. Th. Arnold

title: Striegel Riverside Mixed-Use
description: The residential and commercial complex stands as a pivotal structure in the area, adjacent to the new pedestrian bridge leading to the city center. Its strategic location not only bridges two building lines of differing heights but also symbolizes a connection between urban vibrancy and natural serenity. With deliberate architectural elements, such as the shared natural stone base and carefully positioned openings offering picturesque views of the surrounding landscape, the building encapsulates the essence of harmonizing city living with tranquil riverside allure. Furthermore, its integration of a ground-floor café creates a dynamic social hub, fostering community engagement and enhancing the overall livability of the area. This project not only offers functional spaces but also adds significant value by enriching the urban fabric and fostering a sense of connectivity and vitality in the neighborhood.

title: Hospital Entrance Hall
description: The entrance hall at the Kassel Hospital stands as a vital link between the city-facing plaza, the serene park surroundings and the larger hospital complex. It not only serves as a welcoming hub and information center but also hosts a café, offering visitors a seamless transition between indoor comfort and outdoor relaxation. Designed with a light and transparent facade, the building embodies an innovative approach to climate control. Its wooden louvers create an enchanting interplay of light and shadow, enhancing the tranquil atmosphere inside. Moreover, the prominent canopy extends a warm invitation to visitors, emphasizing the entrance to the plaza. Guided by a series of elegant steel light columns, guests are effortlessly directed to the heart of the facility, where comfort and convenience converge.

title: EAM Headquarters
description: The project's essence lies in seamlessly integrating the expansive building into its diverse urban and natural surroundings, enhancing the workspace's overall quality. Its design ethos prioritizes harmony between the landscape, adjacent areas, and the volume itself, fostering an environment conducive to productivity and well-being. The facade design, particularly in the office sections, embodies a sense of lightness and openness, contributing to a welcoming and inspiring atmosphere. By gracefully blending with the surrounding green spaces, especially towards the southern part of the property, the office wings create an inviting and serene work environment. Additionally, the strategic placement of central facilities facing the main street reflects a thoughtful approach to urban integration. Moreover, the inclusion of renowned artist Brian Clarke's glass facade artwork in the foyer adds a touch of sophistication, symbolizing the company's commitment to excellence and creativity.

title: Crematorium
description: The crematorium project enhances the historical setting of the Kassel Main Cemetery by adding a distinctive yet respectful modern volume. Positioned within the courtyard, it preserves the integrity of the existing layout while introducing a contemporary edge. The design, featuring a translucent facade and symbolic green hues, embodies themes of lightness, hope, and nature, adding depth to its significance. Operating day and night, the building's appearance dynamically evolves with changing daylight, symbolizing the cycle of life and offering solace to visitors. Additionally, the thoughtful integration of illuminated elements symbolizes the transformative journey of remembrance. The strategic placement of weathering steel baffles not only marks entry points but also serves as a subtle guide for visitors, ensuring a compassionate and dignified experience. Overall, this project seamlessly blends modern functionality with timeless symbolism, enriching the cemetery's heritage and providing a comforting space for reflection.

title: St. Bonifatius Kindergarten
description: The new building of St. Boniface Church's kindergarten in stands out for its design and construction with ecological principles, using natural building materials. Its southern facade features a laminated timber structure with wooden windows and sliding sun protection elements, creating a connection to the adjacent playground. Easily accessible from the first floor, the garden becomes an integral part of the children's experience. Designed to harmonize with the neighboring parish house and church, this kindergarten embodies a commitment to sustainability and child-centric design, fostering a nurturing environment for learning and growth.
